2606.16545.title: Can LLM Coding Agents Reason About Time Series?
2606.16545.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly being used for automated decision-making systems in finance, healthcare, or environmental monitoring. Time series data are ubiquitous in these fields, yet hard to process automatically. Can time series be analyzed by LLM agents? We examine three approaches: providing the agent with raw numerical data, using the LLM as a coding agent, or a combination of both. In the coding agent setup, the model iteratively queries the data using Python code. Using two time series understanding benchmarks, we show that agents with code access can outperform models processing raw data by up to 10%. However, even the best performing agent still answers about 22-34% of the questions incorrectly. To get insights into models' strategies and reasoning gaps, we analyze the model outputs with a strong LLM judge. Our analysis reveals that coding agents can select appropriate statistical tests, but often miss important nuances. Meanwhile, models with access to raw data can reach the right conclusions using back-of-the-envelope calculations.
2606.16545.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在金融、醫療保健或環境監測等自動化決策系統中的使用越來越普遍。時間序列數據在這些領域中無處不在，但自動處理卻很困難。時間序列能否被LLM代理分析？我們檢視了三種方法：提供代理原始數據、將LLM用作編碼代理，或兩者的結合。在編碼代理設置中，模型使用Python代碼迭代查詢數據。使用兩個時間序列理解基準，我們顯示具有代碼訪問權限的代理可以比處理原始數據的模型表現更好，最多提高10%。然而，即使是表現最佳的代理，仍然有約22-34%的問題回答錯誤。為了深入了解模型的策略和推理缺口，我們使用強大的LLM評判分析模型輸出。我們的分析揭示，編碼代理能夠選擇適當的統計測試，但往往忽略重要的細微差別。與此同時，具有原始數據訪問權限的模型可以通過粗略計算得出正確的結論。
2606.16545.author: Filip Rechtorík et.al.
2606.16545.authors: Filip Rechtorík, Ondřej Dušek, Zdeněk Kasner
